    <title>ARCH-A.5b: collect shard_count 4 and 8 validation evidence</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
ARCH-A.5 requires the higher-shard validation data needed to test linear scaling and the CPU utilization exit gate. This child owns shard_count=4 and shard_count=8 evidence collection.

ROOT CAUSE
<governing>docs/helix/02-design/system-design/SD-011-pipeline-performance-model.md:9 (SD-011 §9 spike exit gate); parent: niflheim-3bd0fce8 (ARCH-A spike); decomposed parent: niflheim-520db87a.</governing>

DEPENDENCIES
- ARCH-A.1 (per-shard materializer queue)
- ARCH-A.2 (per-shard WAL writers)
- ARCH-A.3 (per-shard Kafka consumer)
- ARCH-A.4 (wipe guard — so the runs at different shard_counts are honest, not silently corrupt)

PROPOSED FIX
Run prism_stress_local for the 32-partition workload (32k×300B realistic, 100×10) at shard_count=4 and shard_count=8. Capture throughput, CPU%, run metadata, logs, and flame graph artifacts under the same docs/helix/04-build/evidence/IP-010/throughput/<timestamp>-arch-a/ evidence directory used for ARCH-A.5. Record shard_count=4 sustained ingest CPU alongside the 11% baseline so the aggregate write-up can evaluate the >25% pass criterion.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not run shard_count=1 or shard_count=2; those belong to a sibling child bead.
- Do not decide success vs lesson.
- Do not file follow-up beads.
- Do not update SD-011 §9 with the binding constraint or next architectural move.
- Do not implement the next architectural step if needed; file as a separate bead.
- Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads.
- Preserve Cross-refs: ADR-061, SD-011 §9.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Evidence for shard_count=4 and shard_count=8 exists under docs/helix/04-build/evidence/IP-010/throughput/ARCH-A/<run-id>/, including rate/cpu data, run metadata, logs, and flame graph artifacts or an explicit per-run note if flame capture is unavailable.
2. Evidence records shard_count=4 sustained ingest CPU% alongside the 11% baseline and includes enough context to evaluate the >25% criterion.
3. Evidence records the workload shape: prism_stress_local, 32 Kafka partitions, 32k rows/s target, 300B realistic rows, 100x10 cardinality.
4. scripts/with-pinned-rust.sh cargo test -p arch-a-validation --release --test arch_a_validation passes.
5. cd benchmarks/throughput && uv run pytest tests/test_arch_a_sweep.py passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
Accepted/unblocked 2026-05-18: operator requested queue unblocking. Removed the non-existent cd cli && go test ./... gate and replaced it with repo-real arch-a-validation and throughput harness checks. Redpanda compose startup was verified after commit 90cd8348.
    </notes>

  <bead id="niflheim-439a49ae">
    <title>ARCH-A/5: linear-scaling validation against prism_stress_local</title>
    <description>
<governing>ADR-061 §"Validation gate", SD-011 §4.2a (0.7× linear scaling target), parent niflheim-3bd0fce8. Depends on niflheim-41af19a2 (ARCH-A/3) and niflheim-7817b555 (ARCH-A/4).</governing>

## Scope

End-to-end validation that ARCH-A delivers the SD-011 §4.2a perf target: `rate(shard_count=N) ≥ 0.7 × N × rate(shard_count=1)` for N ∈ {2, 4, 8} on the prism_stress_local workload (32 Kafka partitions). Capture evidence under `docs/helix/04-build/evidence/IP-010/throughput/`.

## Implementation

### Workload

Use `benchmarks/throughput/src/fast_producer.py` against the local Redpanda + niflheim setup, with the pre-generated 500k-record JSONL fixture (or generate a larger one for longer runs). Topic: `prism_stress_local` with 32 Kafka partitions.

### Test matrix

For each `shard_count ∈ {1, 2, 4, 8}`:

1. Update niflheim config: `[ingest] shard_count = N`.
2. Wipe the data dir (ADR-061: shard_count change requires wipe).
3. Start niflheim. Wait for ready.
4. Drive 5 minutes of sustained load via `fast_producer.py --parallelism 16 --duration 300 --repeat`.
5. Sample niflheim CPU, RSS, /dev/sdd %util, eth0 RX, niflheim proc_write_bytes every 5s (reuse `/tmp/sweep_harness.py` from the prior fast-producer evidence).
6. Capture niflheim observed consume rate via `system.source_progress` (commit_frontier_offset delta).

### Per-step capture

- `step_<shard_count>.json` — producer rate, niflheim observed rate, CPU max/avg, disk %util max, RSS max, proc_write_mbps, net_rx_mbps
- `samples_<shard_count>.csv` — 5s-interval samples
- `fastprod_<shard_count>.json` — raw fast-producer output

### Analysis

Compute the linear-scaling ratio per N:
```
ratio(N) = niflheim_observed_rate(N) / (N × niflheim_observed_rate(1))
```

Target: `ratio(N) >= 0.7` for N ∈ {2, 4, 8}.

### Evidence

Save under `docs/helix/04-build/evidence/IP-010/throughput/<TS>-arch-a-validation-<sha>/`:

- `SUMMARY.md` — comparison table; whether the 0.7× target is met per N; classification of dominant bottleneck if the target is missed (disk saturation, CPU saturation per shard, merge_gate, etc.); recommendation on default `shard_count` post-validation.
- Per-step JSONs + sample CSVs.

### Spike exit gate (per ADR-061)

If validation succeeds: file follow-up beads for (a) per-shard Prometheus metrics, (b) re-shard utility tool, (c) cross-shard tenant fairness if skew was observed.

If validation fails: SUMMARY.md must classify why (e.g., "shard_count=8 still saturates at 12 cores because per-shard WAL writer is now the bound; recommend further sharding by sub-partition or examining bottlenecks downstream of WAL append"). Decide whether to revert ARCH-A or merge it as a stepping stone.

## Acceptance

1. Sweep run for shard_count ∈ {1, 2, 4, 8} completes; evidence directory exists with all per-step artifacts.
2. SUMMARY.md present with the ratio table.
3. **Either:** the 0.7× target is met for at least N ∈ {2, 4}; the spike is judged a success.
4. **Or:** the SUMMARY classifies the next bottleneck and proposes the follow-up direction (per ADR-061 spike exit gate).
5. If success: niflheim.toml default `shard_count` is updated to the post-validation value (likely `num_cores / 2`).
6. The cloud catchup may run alongside or be paused — operator's call; document the choice in SUMMARY.md.

## Out of scope

- Re-shard tool (utility to migrate WAL data between shard layouts).
- Per-shard observability beyond what's needed to interpret the sweep.
- Power-loss recovery validation (covered separately via ADR-060 path, not this bead).

## References

- ADR-061 §"Validation gate", §"Spike exit gate"
- SD-011 §4.2a — roofline equation and 0.7× target
- fast_producer harness: `benchmarks/throughput/src/fast_producer.py`
- Reference sweep harness: prior evidence at `docs/helix/04-build/evidence/IP-010/throughput/20260510T175216Z-fastprod-sweep-2a3cf0af/sweep_harness.py`
    </description>
    <acceptance/>
    <notes>
Unblocked 2026-05-18: the prior Redpanda/AIO blocker was stale after commit 90cd8348 bounded Redpanda test storage with an owned tmpfs mount. Verified the exact compose path with: docker compose -f benchmarks/competitive/docker-compose.yml -f benchmarks/throughput/docker-compose.override.yml --profile multi-stack up -d redpanda; docker exec competitive-redpanda-1 rpk cluster info. The container became ready and was cleaned up. Remaining work is the ARCH-A validation itself.
    </notes>
    <labels>spec:SD-011, perf, throughput, architecture, sharding, benchmark, area:perf, kind:validation, arch-a, child</labels>

  <bead id="niflheim-da8fb4a4">
    <title>Fix S3 cold-tier timeout-cancel feedback loop: configurable attempt timeout for large WAL segment uploads</title>
    <description>
S3 cold-tier writes against Garage are entering a self-reinforcing
failure loop. The proximate cause is that `S3ObjectStore::with_retry`
(`crates/niflheim-object-store/src/s3/store.rs:233`) wraps every
attempt in `tokio::time::timeout(self.config.attempt_timeout, f())`.
When the timer fires, tokio **drops the inner future** — and the
inner future is the in-flight `aws-sdk-s3` `PutObject` call. Dropping
a hyper request mid-body teardowns the TCP connection while the
request body is still being streamed.

Confirmed from the Garage side: 100% of last-hour warnings on
`eldir.azgaard.home:3900` are
`WARN garage_api_common::generic_server: Response: error 500 Internal
Server Error, Internal error (Hyper error): error reading a body
from connection` — 100% sourced from this niflheim node
(192.168.2.118). 285 PUT / 79 DELETE / 32 GET against
`/niflheim-cold/wal/segment-NNNNNNNN-offN-N.wal` in the same window.

Garage itself is healthy (4 TB free, "fully operational", 4.75×
compression on data, RPC and admin sockets bound and responsive).

**Why it triggers**

- `S3Config::default().attempt_timeout = Duration::from_secs(60)`
  (`s3/config.rs:40`).
- WAL segments are 1 GiB (`segment_seal_bytes = 1073741824` in the
  sindri-dev `niflheim.toml`); 60 s to PUT 1 GiB requires sustained
  ~17 MB/s end-to-end. Under any contention (concurrent PUTs, ZFS
  background work on Garage, Garage GC) the budget is missed.
- Timeout fires → future cancelled → connection torn down → Garage
  records 500 → `is_transient_sdk_error` returns true → retry up to
  `max_retries=3` → same fate → 4 attempts per segment all fail.
- Each cancelled attempt leaves bytes in niflheim's kernel send
  buffer (~100 MB across ~63 concurrent stuck sockets observed live)
  and opens new connections rather than waiting for the prior one to
  drain.
- Every retry-WARN line is exported by the file telemetry exporter,
  contributing materially to a 810 GB on-disk OTel volume in the
  sindri-dev install — directly motivating the sibling bead
  `niflheim-280de9c9` (rotation).

**Candidate approaches** — pick during execution, document choice:

1. **Plumb `attempt_timeout` and `total_deadline` through
   `niflheim.toml`** under `[storage]` (e.g.,
   `s3_attempt_timeout_secs`, `s3_total_deadline_secs`) and raise
   defaults to something realistic for 1 GiB segments (e.g., 600 s
   / 1800 s). Minimal code change; lets operators tune without a
   rebuild.
2. **Make `attempt_timeout` proportional to expected body size** —
   compute per-call based on segment/chunk bytes (e.g.,
   `base + bytes / min_throughput`). Removes the operator-tuning
   burden but harder to bound correctly.
3. **Switch the WAL `put_object` paths to S3 multipart upload**
   (`UploadPart` + `CompleteMultipartUpload`). Each part has its
   own retry budget; on part failure the SDK can resume without
   re-uploading the whole body. This is the right long-term fix
   for objects ≥ ~100 MiB. Larger change; touches
   `crates/niflheim-object-store/src/s3/wal_chunk_store_impl.rs`
   and the chunk-keyed coalesce path.
4. **Graceful-cancel** — when `with_retry` times out, instead of
   dropping the future, issue `AbortMultipartUpload` (or `Close`
   the underlying connection cleanly) so Garage doesn't observe a
   mid-body teardown. Helps server-side bookkeeping even when
   another fix is the primary remedy.

Approaches (1) and (3) are non-overlapping — (1) is a quick win, (3)
is the structural fix. The expected delivery is **(1) first as a
hotfix**, then (3) as a follow-up if multipart is judged worth the
churn.

**In-scope files (for approach 1):**
- `crates/niflheim-object-store/src/s3/config.rs` (defaults; optional
  builders already exist)
- `crates/niflheim-object-store/src/wal_runtime.rs:112` (read new
  fields from `StorageConfig`, call `with_attempt_timeout` /
  `with_total_deadline`)
- `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/storage.rs` (add the two new
  fields with Serde defaults preserving current behavior)
- New tests covering the wiring (config → S3Config) and a unit test
  asserting that with a large `attempt_timeout` an in-flight
  upload does **not** get dropped before completing — use a mock
  `S3 Client` or a slow `tokio::time` controlled future.

**Out-of-scope (file separately if needed):**
- OTel file rotation (`niflheim-280de9c9` already covers it).
- Operator action to truncate the existing 810 GB of debris.
- Garage-side tuning (the server is healthy; no action needed there).
- Reducing `segment_seal_bytes` — that's a runtime config change the
  operator can do today, not a code change.

**Why not "just bump the default in config.rs":** because the right
budget is workload-dependent (1 GiB segments need ≥ 5 min on a
contended LAN; a future deployment with 256 MiB segments would be
over-provisioned at 5 min). Operator-tunable via TOML is the right
ergonomics.

**Rollback behavior:** New TOML fields are optional with Serde
defaults matching today's hardcoded `Duration::from_secs(60) /
Duration::from_secs(300)` — reverting is a config edit, not a binary
roll.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `cargo test -p niflheim-object-store --lib s3` passes,
   including a new test that asserts:
     a. `S3Config` populated from a `StorageConfig` with
        `s3_attempt_timeout_secs = 600` produces
        `attempt_timeout == Duration::from_secs(600)`.
     b. Omitting the new field preserves the legacy default
        (`Duration::from_secs(60)`).
2. `cargo test -p niflheim-core --lib config::storage` passes,
   including a round-trip test (`toml::from_str` →
   `toml::to_string` → equality) for the new fields.
3. `cargo build --workspace` succeeds, no new warnings introduced.
4. `cargo clippy -p niflheim-object-store -p niflheim-core --
   -D warnings` passes.
5. `grep -E 's3_attempt_timeout_secs|s3_total_deadline_secs'
   crates/niflheim-core/src/config/storage.rs` returns the two new
   fields wired into the public struct.
6. End-to-end smoke (manual or scripted) on sindri-dev: with the
   new TOML knob set to `s3_attempt_timeout_secs = 600` and a
   restart, observe in `journalctl`/OTel logs that the
   `niflheim_object_store::s3::store` WARN rate for "attempt timed
   out" falls to ~0 over a 5-minute window with steady ingest.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:storage, area:wal, kind:reliability, spec:ADR-032, spec:ADR-051, phase:iterate, observability</labels>
    <metadata spec-id="ADR-051" base-rev="1a7df6cebd321748d08ca19113f3a5792860c006" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260528T042621-ada3a65f"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="ADR-051" path="docs/helix/02-design/adrs/ADR-051-cold-tier-object-storage-backends.md">ADR-051: Cold Tier Object Storage Backends</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

    <title>Recovery state machine: typed errors + streaming restage (Phase 2)</title>
    <description>
<governing>SD-009 §3.1 R-009-REC-001 (no silent in-RAM replay) + §3.1.5 recovery state machine.</governing>

## Scope

Implement the seven recovery paths from SD-009 §3.1.5 explicitly. Each is its own typed error or restage path:

| Failure point | Detection | Action |
|---|---|---|
| Mid-IPC-append (writer crash before EOS) | Trailing partial message detected on read | Truncate at last valid message; consolidator resumes appending |
| Mid-merge (no Delta commit) | `staging_consumed_extents` lacks the row for the merge attempt | Re-enqueue; merge re-runs against same extents |
| Post-Delta-commit pre-bookmark | `staging_consumed_extents` row exists with delta_version ≥ table version | Idempotent: write `Drained` manifest entry on recovery |
| Post-bookmark pre-rotation | Manifest is `Drained` for some extents but not for new ones | Resume: append new extents, rotate when triggered |
| Mid-compaction (Phase 3 only) | Compacted Parquet partial; manifest still `Drained` | Discard partial Parquet; retry compaction |
| Disk full | StagingQuotaController hard pressure | SQLSTATE 53100 + propagate via WAL retention |
| Corrupt staging file | CRC32C / IPC magic mismatch on read | Typed `StagingCorrupt` error; manifest entry quarantined; merge fails to dead-letter queue |

NO recovery path is allowed to silently re-decode WAL into in-RAM RecordBatches.

## Non-goals

- Compaction recovery (Phase 3 sibling).
- Cross-tenant recovery prioritization (Phase 4).

## Acceptance

1. Each recovery path has a unit test exercising the trigger and validating the action.
2. Typed errors: `StagingError::CorruptFile`, `StagingError::PartialAppend`, `StagingError::ManifestStale`, etc. — enumerated and documented.
3. Integration test (with chaos harness from p2_chaos): kill-restart cycle through each path produces deterministic recovery.
4. SD-009 §3.1.5 table updated to reference test names.
5. clippy + lefthook clean.

## Suite

scripts/with-pinned-rust.sh cargo test -p niflheim-delta-writer --lib source::persistent::recovery && scripts/with-pinned-rust.sh cargo test -p niflheim-integration-tests staging::recovery

## Files touched

crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/source/persistent/recovery.rs (new)
docs/helix/02-design/system-design/SD-009-pgwire-ddl-and-wal-delta-apply.md (test refs in §3.1.5)

## Depends on

p2_manifest, p2_persistent_source, p2_bookmarks_extent_identity.
    </description>

All seven recovery paths have unit tests; typed errors enumerated; integration test through chaos harness deterministic; SD-009 §3.1.5 refs tests.

when stacked with planning + commit.

   under each compression setting and report per-merge wall time and
   data_dir delta MB. Choose the default that has the best
